title: LlamaCppRuntime LoRA hot-swap (per-request --lora; mount/unmount without process restart)
goal: Implement LoraStackOps for LlamaCppRuntime via the per-request --lora mechanism shipped in llama.cpp 2025 (multi-LoRA support). mount loads a LoRA adapter into a llama-cpp-2 LlamaModel without unloading the base; per-request lora_overrides honored in generate; unmount removes from the active set. NO process restart.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/llama_cpp/lora_impl.rs implementing LoraStackOps; integration with generate.rs to apply per-request overrides; tests (env-gated).
unblock_conditions: MT-064 LoraStackOps trait + MT-074 generate path merged.
implementation_notes: Per AC-LLAMACPP-ADAPTER + AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.a (LoRA hot-swap PRODUCTION) and refinement.json inference_technique_matrix INF-1: llama.cpp 2025 supports multi-LoRA via `llama_lora_adapter_init` + per-context `llama_lora_adapter_set` (scale param) without unloading the base model. Implementation: LlamaCppLoraStack holds `HashMap<LoraId, llama_cpp_2::LoraAdapter>` plus a per-context active set. mount: validate LoraDescriptor.base_model_compat matches the model's base tag (compare LoadSpec.base_model_tag); load GGUF LoRA via llama_cpp_2 LoraAdapter constructor (path + sha256 verification); set_lora_adapter on the active context with the provided strength. unmount: llama_lora_adapter_remove (or set strength to 0.0 and drop from active set). set_strength: call llama_lora_adapter_set with new scale. swap (atomic replace): for each new entry, ensure it can mount; if any fails, roll back to previous snapshot; on success, batch-apply via a single context lock. Per-request override path: GenerateRequest.lora_overrides Vec<LoraId> — generate.rs uses these for the call duration; if a LoraId is not currently mounted, mount it transiently (with explicit failure if mount fails). License tagging mandatory (LoraDescriptor.license_tag is non-Optional). Tests (env-gated, requires a small LoRA file beside the GGUF): mount LoRA, generate with override, verify output differs from base; unmount, verify output reverts; swap with a failing entry rolls back; per-request override on non-mounted LoRA works (transient mount). FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-LORA-MOUNT / UNMOUNT events emitted.
risk_if_missed: LoRA hot-swap (INF-1) ships with broken LlamaCpp path; AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.a fails on default adapter.
validator_focus: No process restart on mount/unmount; base-model-tag validation; atomic swap rollback on partial failure; per-request override path covered.

minimum_controls: Process-restart-free invariant: PID before and after mount/unmount/swap unchanged (test asserts std::process::id()).

implementation_reality_20260521.summary: MT-076 implemented the safe subset supported by locked llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 and records the true multi-LoRA/event gaps for validator review instead of claiming an unsupported multi-LoRA pass.
details: The original MT text assumed llama.cpp 2025 multi-LoRA and per-request --lora CLI semantics. Local research against locked llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 found the safe Rust wrapper only exposes one adapter per context via LlamaContext::lora_adapter_set and lora_adapter_remove; upstream C supports multi-adapter arrays, but the wrapper does not expose the raw handles safely.
Implementation therefore does not fake multi-LoRA. It enforces at most one mounted/applied LoRA for llama.cpp and returns explicit LoraStackError messages when more are requested.
Per-request behavior is implemented by applying the selected mounted adapter to the fresh generation context for the call duration, not by shelling out through llama.cpp server CLI --lora flags.

Native adapter Drop uses the locked wrapper representation plus llama-cpp-sys-2::llama_adapter_lora_free because llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 has no Drop implementation for LlamaLoraAdapter. Each NativeLoraAdapter owns an Arc<NativeLlamaCppBackend> so the underlying model outlives adapter free.

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.summary: Implemented LlamaCppRuntime LoRA stack support for the current llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 safe wrapper: loaded llama.cpp models expose LoraStackOps, descriptor/base/sha/license validation runs before adapter activation, generation honors mounted lora_overrides on the per-request native context, unmounted overrides fail before decoding, KV-prefix plus LoRA is rejected because prefix handles are not LoRA-scoped, single active LoRA is enforced honestly, adapter lifetime is pinned to the owning native backend, and mount/unmount/swap do not restart the process. This is supporting implementation/test evidence only; WP remains In Progress and Integration Validator PASS remains pending.
corrected_contract_reality: The original MT text assumed llama.cpp 2025 multi-LoRA and per-request --lora CLI semantics. Local research against locked llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 found the safe Rust wrapper only exposes one adapter per context via LlamaContext::lora_adapter_set and lora_adapter_remove; upstream C supports multi-adapter arrays, but the wrapper does not expose the raw handles safely.
Implementation therefore does not fake multi-LoRA. It enforces at most one mounted/applied LoRA for llama.cpp and returns explicit LoraStackError messages when more are requested.
Per-request behavior is implemented by applying the selected mounted adapter to the fresh generation context for the call duration, not by shelling out through llama.cpp server CLI --lora flags.

Native adapter Drop uses the locked wrapper representation plus llama-cpp-sys-2::llama_adapter_lora_free because llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 has no Drop implementation for LlamaLoraAdapter. Each NativeLoraAdapter owns an Arc<NativeLlamaCppBackend> so the underlying model outlives adapter free.
high_roi_additions: Added explicit single-LoRA stack enforcement at mount, swap, and generation selection time so the runtime cannot advertise impossible multi-LoRA state under the current wrapper.
Added KV-prefix plus LoRA rejection in both preflight and context-application paths to close the concurrent default-LoRA mount race identified by review.
Added native adapter lifetime hardening so mounted adapters keep the owning model alive during native free and generation guards keep adapters alive until after context drop.
Added native test serialization guard matching existing llama.cpp KV tests to avoid false BackendAlreadyInitialized failures under Rust test concurrency.
Added env-gated negative tests for second mount precheck and unload while mounted; they compile and will exercise real LoRA paths when HANDSHAKE_TEST_LORA_PATH is provided.

proof_commands.note: Initial RED failed because loaded llama.cpp models exposed no LoRA stack and unmounted lora_overrides decoded instead of failing.
Ran after review hardening and final lifetime/precheck edits.
6 passed, 0 failed. Real LoRA fixture-specific tests are env-gated and returned early because HANDSHAKE_TEST_LORA_PATH was not set in this session.

supporting_reviews.summary: Confirmed locked llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 exposes only one safe LoRA adapter per context while upstream C supports multi-adapter arrays.

Found KV-prefix/LoRA race, missing stack mutation single-LoRA enforcement, native adapter lifetime leak/free risk, and negative-test gaps. Final implementation added apply-time KV-prefix rejection, mount/swap single-LoRA enforcement, explicit adapter free, and expanded tests.

Found post-fix native adapter/model drop-order risk and mount precheck ordering issue. Final implementation makes NativeLoraAdapter retain the owning NativeLlamaCppBackend and rejects second active mounts before adapter load.

Confirmed the correct governance state for MT-076 is completed implementation evidence with validator_verdict and integration_validator_verdict still PENDING, and WP ready_for_validation remains false.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-076; this record must not be treated as an authoritative WP pass.

True multi-LoRA on llama.cpp is not implemented under the locked safe Rust wrapper. Supporting it later requires a wrapper patch/fork or a safe upstream API for llama_set_adapters_lora with multiple handles.

No real LoRA fixture was configured in this session. Native load/generate tests exercised a real GGUF model, but real LoRA mount/application/unload negative paths remain env-gated on HANDSHAKE_TEST_LORA_PATH.

FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-LORA-MOUNT / UNMOUNT emission is not wired in the current ModelRuntime LoRA surface; no existing model-runtime event hook was present to reuse during this MT. Validator should decide whether this belongs in a later event-ledger MT or requires reopening this scope.
Native adapter free relies on locked llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 layout knowledge. If the crate later adds Drop for LlamaLoraAdapter or changes representation, this manual free path must be revisited.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: LoRA hot-swap implemented honestly within wrapper limits: single-LoRA per context enforced, sha256 + base-tag + license validated, no process restart, KV-prefix race blocked. Multi-LoRA contract-text and FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-LORA-MOUNT/UNMOUNT emission are deferred per builder disclosure.
